templates: {name} has worked the stable in {town} for {years} years. {pronoun_cap} knows more about the horses passing through than about the people riding them, which suits {pronoun_obj} fine. {personality_line} {hook}
Ask {name} about an animal and {pronoun} will talk for an hour. Ask about people and {pronoun} will give you a shrug and a price. {personality_line} {hook}
{name} came to this work after a riding accident that ended a different career. {pronoun_cap} doesn't elaborate. {personality_line} {hook}

personality_lines.cautious: {pronoun_cap} checks every horse's shoes before taking the animal in. A lame horse is everyone's problem.
personality_lines.loyal: The animals in {pronoun_pos} care eat before {pronoun} does.
personality_lines.curious: {pronoun_cap} can tell a horse's origin, breeding, and recent treatment from a five-minute inspection.
personality_lines.cynical: {pronoun_cap} judges a person's character by how they treat their horse. Most people don't come out well.
personality_lines.cheerful: {pronoun_cap} talks to the animals. The animals, {pronoun} maintains, talk back.
personality_lines.patient: A spooked horse will calm down. {pronoun_cap} has never found one that didn't, given enough time.
personality_lines.brave: {pronoun_cap} pulled two horses from a stable fire with {pronoun_pos} bare hands. {pronoun_cap} rarely mentions it.
personality_lines.generous: Travelers who look genuinely poor sometimes find their animal has been fed and watered for free.
personality_lines.suspicious: {pronoun_cap} remembers every horse that has come through and where it went.
personality_lines.melancholy: There is a particular stall {pronoun} keeps empty. {pronoun_cap} won't say why.

hooks: A horse was left here two weeks ago with full payment and no return date. The owner has not come back.
{pronoun_cap} noticed unusual marks on a recent traveler's horse — military brands that shouldn't be this far from the capital.
Something has been getting into the stable at night. No animal is missing. Nothing is disturbed. But something has been there.
{pronoun_cap} overheard a conversation {pronoun} wasn't meant to while mucking out the far stall.
A horse arrived last week that {pronoun} recognizes — but its rider is someone {pronoun} has never seen before.
